The Master Builders association
will not find II an easy task to ar
range a permanent wage scale for
the next two years. The latter has
never been drawn to the attention of
the building trades of the city nor
in any communication to the trades
council.


OF course, the trades council will
not hesitate to appoint committee
TO confer witt. the master builders
in the event of such a conference be-
ing desired by the employers, but the
outlook is that no agreement will be
reacned.


Members of the council stated this
morning that it would be impossible
TO enter into an agreement Of this
kind as the unions concerned would
have to have a guarantee that the
cost Of living and rent would not go
up within the two years of the con-
tract.
